metadata.description: Curated external reference dataset for Long-EZ Model 61. Each entry cites its source. Used by Phase 4+ validation to compare computed values against published specifications. This is the single authoritative source of published specs, wind tunnel data, and community build measurements.
metadata.datum_note: All fuselage stations (FS) in this file use the PUBLISHED Long-EZ datum from RAF CP-29/CP-31 (nose = FS 0). Internal code FS values differ by ~45.5 inches. Use config.geometry.to_published_datum() to convert.

raf-cp29.title: Long-EZ Owner's Manual (CP-29)
raf-cp29.author: Rutan Aircraft Factory
raf-cp29.notes: Primary pilot operating handbook and specification document issued with Long-EZ plans. Contains performance data, weight and balance limits, CG envelope, and operating limitations.

raf-cp31.title: Long-EZ Plans Section IIA — Geometry and Aerodynamics (CP-31)
raf-cp31.author: Rutan Aircraft Factory
raf-cp31.notes: Construction plans including dimensioned drawings and geometry tables. Source for wing span, canard span, chord lengths, and area reference values. Published aspect ratio uses semi-span reference area convention.

roncz-wt.title: Roncz R1145MS Canard Airfoil Wind Tunnel Report
roncz-wt.author: John R. Roncz
roncz-wt.notes: Wind tunnel test results for the R1145MS airfoil developed to replace the dangerous GU25-5(11)8. Data collected at Purdue low-speed tunnel. Critical safety reference — the R1145MS prevents pitch-down moments from rain-induced contamination that caused fatal accidents with the original airfoil.

eppler-report.title: Eppler 1230 Airfoil Data — UIUC Airfoil Database
eppler-report.author: Richard Eppler; UIUC Applied Aerodynamics Group
eppler-report.notes: Profile coordinates and wind tunnel characteristics for Eppler 1230. Long-EZ main wing uses a modified version with trailing-edge reflex for pitch stability. UIUC database entry compiled from Eppler's published reports.

csa-newsletter.title: Canard Safety Authority / Central States Association Newsletters
csa-newsletter.author: Central States Association
csa-newsletter.notes: Builder-submitted weigh-in records and build experience reports compiled in CSA newsletters. Data spans multiple decades of Long-EZ builds. Quality varies; treated as community-tier confidence.

builder-weigh-in.title: Type Club Builder Weigh-In Records
builder-weigh-in.author: Various Long-EZ builders / EAA chapters
builder-weigh-in.notes: Weight and balance data collected at fly-ins and EAA chapter meetings. Anonymized to protect builder privacy. Represents real-world as-built aircraft vs. design estimates.

wing_span_in.notes: Total wingspan 26.4 ft = 316.8 in. Matches config.geometry.wing_span exactly.

wing_area_sqft.notes: Published reference area of 94.2 sqft uses RAF's semi-panel convention (one side only, excluding strake center body). Code computes 110 sqft using full trapezoidal planform — see aspect_ratio entry for full convention explanation.

canard_span_in.notes: Total canard span 12.25 ft = 147.0 in. Matches config.geometry.canard_span.

canard_area_sqft.notes: Published canard reference area. Code computes 15.57 sqft — within 0.2% of published, agreement is good.

aspect_ratio.notes: CONVENTION DISCREPANCY — NOT A BUG: Published AR = 7.3 uses RAF's reference area convention (~94.2 sqft per semi-panel). Code computes AR = 6.34 using full trapezoidal wing area (~110 sqft). Formula: AR = span^2 / area. With 316.8 in span and 94.2 sqft reference: AR = (26.4^2) / 94.2 = 7.39 (rounds to 7.3 in plans). With full trapezoidal area: AR = (26.4^2) / 110 = 6.34. Both are arithmetically correct under their respective conventions. Phase 4 validation should compare computed AR against 6.34 (full planform), not 7.3.

max_gross_weight_lb.notes: FAA-approved maximum gross weight for Long-EZ Model 61. Hard limit — not a design target.

empty_weight_lb.notes: Published empty weight is a typical value; actual empty weights vary from 800 to 920 lb depending on propulsion package, avionics, and builder choices. See community_builds for measured values.

useful_load_lb.notes: Derived from max_gross_weight - empty_weight (1425 - 850). Actual useful load depends on as-built empty weight.

cg_range_fwd_fs.notes: Forward CG limit per RAF CP-29. Published datum (nose = FS 0). Internal code equivalent: 99.0 + datum_offset_in.

cg_range_aft_fs.notes: Aft CG limit per RAF CP-29. Published datum (nose = FS 0). Internal code equivalent: 104.0 + datum_offset_in.

neutral_point_fs.notes: Neutral point (aerodynamic center of the full configuration) at FS 108 in published Long-EZ datum. Code computes NP at FS ~153.5 in internal coordinates; applying datum_offset_in = 45.5 yields FS 108.0 published. The 2-inch tolerance [106, 110] reflects typical canard configuration sensitivity. This is the PRIMARY validation anchor for Phase 4.

cruise_speed_ktas.notes: Published cruise speed at 8000 ft with Lycoming O-235 at 75% power. Actual cruise speeds reported in 155-175 KTAS range depending on installation and cowling fairings.

stall_speed_ktas.notes: Published canard stall speed (the canard stalls first, providing pitch-down warning before main wing stall). At gross weight, gear down. The Roncz R1145MS canard is essential for predictable stall behavior.

range_nm.notes: Published range with standard 52-gallon total fuel (26 gal per strake), 45-minute IFR reserve. Actual range varies with pilot weight, cruise altitude, and fuel loading.

vne_ktas.notes: Never-exceed speed per RAF CP-29. Hard structural limit. Flutter margin per 14 CFR 23.629 requires demonstrated flutter-free to 1.2 * Vne = 240 KTAS minimum.

static_margin_pct.page_table: Derived from NP and CG aft limit
static_margin_pct.notes: Static margin = (NP - CG_aft) / MAC * 100. Published NP=108, CG_aft=104, MAC not specified. 5-20% is the safe range for canard configurations. 12% is derived from the published NP and CG aft limit: (108-104)/32 * 100 where 32 is approximate strake-free chord. Tolerance 3% MAC is generous to accommodate MAC calculation differences. For accuracy report: Phase 5 calibrated margins yield static_margin ~1.9% which reflects CG aft limit placement at 7.65% forward of NP.

roncz_r1145ms.description: Roncz R1145MS — Long-EZ canard replacement airfoil (SAFETY CRITICAL)

cl_max.notes: Maximum lift coefficient at design Reynolds number. The R1145MS maintains CLmax even with rain-induced surface roughness — key design requirement over GU25-5(11)8.

cm_zero.notes: Zero-lift pitching moment coefficient. Negative value indicates nose-down tendency at zero lift. Canard configuration uses this for stability — canard must carry positive load.

alpha_zero_lift_deg.notes: Zero-lift angle of attack. Matches config.aero_limits.canard_alpha_0L = -3.0.

cd_min.notes: Minimum profile drag coefficient at design Re. Used for section drag polar construction.

eppler_1230.description: Eppler 1230 (Modified) — Long-EZ main wing airfoil with trailing-edge reflex
eppler_1230.facility: Stuttgart Wind Tunnel / UIUC database compilation

cl_max.notes: CLmax for base Eppler 1230. Long-EZ modification adds trailing-edge reflex (2.5%) which slightly reduces CLmax but improves pitch stability. config.aero_limits.wing_clmax = 1.45.

cm_zero.notes: Zero-lift pitching moment. The reflex modification in the Long-EZ version reduces nose-down tendency, contributing to longitudinal trim without requiring large canard download.

alpha_zero_lift_deg.notes: Zero-lift angle of attack for base Eppler 1230. Matches config.aero_limits.wing_alpha_0L = -2.0.

cd_min.notes: Minimum profile drag coefficient. Lower than R1145MS due to wing operating at lower lift coefficient in cruise.

community_builds.notes: Lycoming O-235 installation, basic VFR avionics. Weighed at annual condition inspection. CG in published datum.
O-235 with upgraded Garmin avionics panel. Slightly heavier than typical due to additional electronics. CSA Newsletter Vol 18.
Lightweight build — minimal avionics, no autopilot. Forward CG due to light aft equipment. Weighed at EAA chapter fly-in.
O-320 conversion (150 HP) — heavier engine moves CG aft. Builder reported this as near aft limit at light fuel load. CSA Newsletter Vol 22.
Standard O-235 build, dual-seat configuration with rear seat. Mid-range weight consistent with published typical value of 850 lb.
